verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the F1 subunit B of ATP synthase, a core catalytic component of the inner mitochondrial membrane. It is a mitochondrial matrix-facing protein integrated into the inner membrane as part of the multi-subunit ATP synthase complex. It has no transmembrane domain anchoring it to the plasma membrane, no GPI anchor, no outer-leaflet lipidation, and no documented surface trafficking. It is not a secreted protein, not a TM-precursor of a shed ligand, not covalently tethered to a surface receptor, and has no documented PM pool even under cell-state stress, cancer conditions, cell death, or tissue-restricted contexts. ATP synthase subunits are canonically intramitochondrial and are not targets of surface-directed therapeutics.
